content: Sesame oil, peanut oil, and avocado oil are recommended for frying and sautéing due to their high smoke points.
Avocado oil is preferred over sesame and peanut oils for frying due to its lower inflammatory response.
Coconut oil can be used for sautéing and baking due to its high smoke point and ability to be consumed raw.
Flax seed oil, especially unrefined, is highly recommended for consumption due to its high Omega-3 to Omega-6 ratio and anti-inflammatory properties.
Macadamia nut oil is suitable for baking and cooking at lower temperatures due to its one-to-one Omega-3 to Omega-6 ratio and low smoke point.
Olive oil has a higher Omega-6 to Omega-3 ratio and is less easily assimilated by the body compared to monounsaturated fats like macadamia nut oil.
Coconut oil is beneficial for energy utilization and can be used for sautéing, baking, or as a snack, thanks to its medium-chain triglycerides and unique metabolic processes.
